steps.title: Find the technology gap
steps.content: The passage first defines artificial leaves as silicon-based solar cells that imitate photosynthesis by using catalysts to split water into hydrogen and oxygen gas. It then shifts to the main limitation: the technology is attracting interest, but it is not yet commercially viable as a large-scale energy source.

steps.title: Match the main idea
steps.content:
• Choice A: This matches both halves of the passage. Artificial leaves are promising, but scientists still need to improve storage, transportation, distribution, cost, and efficiency before the devices can be widely used as an energy source.
  • Choice B: This contradicts the passage because it says artificial leaves have recently increased in commercial use. The passage says the technology is not yet commercially viable.
  • Choice C: This uses a detail about splitting water molecules, but the passage never says artificial leaves do this more efficiently than plants do.
  • Choice D: This mentions the photosynthesis comparison, but it adds a goal of long-term commercial energy storage that the passage does not state.
The answer is A.
